title: GAO flags satellite costs, launch risks in Space Force portfolio
summary: The Government Accountability Office (GAO) has raised concerns about rising costs and launch risks for missile-warning satellites in the U.S. Space Force portfolio. Workforce reductions and gaps in digital engineering could further delay national security launches.

title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket launched a mission to reboost NASA's Swift astrophysics spacecraft, potentially marking the final flight of the Pegasus XL rocket.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a Chinese satellite manufacturer and affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million in a significant funding round. This marks one of the largest single investments for a Chinese commercial satellite company.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is pivoting from rooftop applications to focus on space-based commercialization. The company aims to supply thin-film solar panels for orbital data centers and large satellite constellations.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet’s German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. The launch highlights Isar Aerospace's growing role in the European space industry.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, has secured $10 million in funding from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The investment aims to support the company's growth and international presence.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the benefits and challenges of multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single terminal for satellite communications. The conversation highlights efforts to enhance the resilience of satellite communications.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: The final Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a payload of Amazon's LEO satellites. This mission marked the last time the Atlas 5 would carry a satellite payload.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A report attributes Boeing's CST-100 Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's insufficient oversight. The findings highlight systemic challenges in the spacecraft's development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications for large and complex satellite constellations. The changes are intended to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' to handle growing demand.

title: America 250: From 1776 to the moon and beyond (A Space.com series)
summary: Space.com reflects on U.S. space achievements since 1776 and explores future ambitions as America celebrates its 250th anniversary. The series highlights historical milestones and potential advancements in space exploration.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 217 — America in Space
summary: Episode 217 of This Week In Space podcast discusses the progression of American space efforts. The episode features hosts Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: Human flight was still 7 years away in 1776. Now, we're headed back to the moon
summary: The article reflects on humanity's progress in flight since 1776, noting that human spaceflight was still decades away at that time. It highlights the rapid advancement to modern space exploration, including plans to return to the moon.

title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
summary: This week offers a rare opportunity to spot Uranus in the night sky as Mars passes nearby, making it easier for skywatchers to locate the seventh planet. The event provides a unique chance for amateur astronomers to observe Uranus with minimal effort.

title: Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?
summary: The news discusses the potential for humans or AI-powered humanoid robots to explore Saturn's moon Titan in the future. It questions whether humans will set foot on Titan or if robots will undertake the mission instead.

title: 'Independence Day' at 30: Roland Emmerich & Dean Devlin talk blowing up the White House and crafting a true sci-fi classic (interview)
summary: An interview celebrates the 30th anniversary of the film 'Independence Day,' with directors Roland Emmerich and Dean Devlin discussing its iconic scenes and cultural impact. The conversation highlights the movie's legacy as a sci-fi classic.

title: 1 private spacecraft intercepts another on Space Force's groundbreaking 'Victus Haze' mission
summary: A U.S. Space Force mission, 'Victus Haze,' successfully completed its first tactical intercept of an orbital target using two satellites. This marks a groundbreaking achievement in private spacecraft interception.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward a planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for a Starship launch from Cape Canaveral, Florida, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are actively working to meet this timeline.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company is prioritizing rapid restoration to resume operations.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Several orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled for its first mission since 2021 to air launch the Swift Boost Mission, aiming to save a NASA space telescope. The mission highlights the continued use of air-launched rockets for space operations.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help scientists study the galaxy's structure and dynamics.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's space launches include six orbital missions worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified topic related to the space industry.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to engage the industrial community and discuss implementation strategies.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It emphasizes the innovation in robotic technology for space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and selecting materials capable of withstanding the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on these innovative minds to ensure mission success.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive component in the space industry. The image is not described in detail.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to stronger partnerships with Middle Eastern space companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for collaboration in the space industry.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly delivered imagery of a Rocket Lab spacecraft within 61 hours of receiving an order from SSC, exceeding the 72-hour deadline. The rapid execution highlights True Anomaly's responsiveness in space operations.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space for its second military satellite, Aurora, as part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to validate new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge announced plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The approach aims to minimize expenses by utilizing locally sourced materials for support structures and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and revealed new contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims to achieve monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: NASA's CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions are preparing to land on the Moon, with various landers involved. The initiative aims to support lunar exploration and scientific research.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satellite communications market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into the satcom sector.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Sweden's Esrange Space Center in 2028 under a new franchise model. The company aims to expand its global launch capabilities with this partnership.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service across key latitudes following a successful ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites, potentially as early as late 2026. This marks a significant win for the German launcher currently in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intention to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in Japan's space sector. The deal highlights Mitsubishi Electric's strategic expansion in the space industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully tested a direct-to-device (D2D) call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation to assess connectivity for emergency and first responders. The test highlights potential advancements in satellite-based emergency communications.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications and the dominant apps driving satellite traffic as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum transactions. The move aims to streamline financial restructuring amid industry challenges.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs has ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The contract was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president to oversee daily operations and expand manufacturing capabilities. The move aims to drive growth and investment in the company's space communications sector.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.
